mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and investment, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning as supply-side bottlenecks threaten corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains above 3% while the federal funds rate stays below 4.5%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 315 and FEDFUNDS < 4.5, or the predictive IC turns positive over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decelerate faster than GDP, signals weakening consumer momentum that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below GDPC1 annualized growth rate
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 48% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a robust supply-demand balance that supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when real rates are negative.
